Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

My main concern with this patch is that it adds a bunch of code that is used in normal operation, but is really only needed for benchmarking with obdfilter-survey and echo_client. 

It would really be better if this code could be added only for obdfilter-survey and live in the echo_client module.

Patch Set 1:

Yes this patch is me
I do have a follow that improves buffer i/o performance that is measurable when the page cache operations become cpu bound ... my testing shows about 20% lower cpu and similarly improved bandwidth.
On the other hand there is 
LU-12071 osd-ldiskfs: bypass pagecache if requested
which may further limit the usefulness of improving page cache performance (by such a little amount).

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

room for improvement...

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I think this is a wrong approach, there is already infrastructure to preserve environment between systemcalls - cl_env_get(), so that there will be no need to reallocate pages each time.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

(8 comments)

(2 comments)

2 style warning(s).

Patch Set 2:

More of an RFC than anything.
The only advantage here is the slight boost in performance for buffer I/O. Also you can toggle the  read_cache_enable / writethrough_cache_enable values without hitting an LBUG ... so there is that.

Patch Set 2: Verified-1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

While this can be made to work for RHEL7 kernels as is .. going forward this change requires re-implementing a few (at least 3) variants of the page mapping data structure to be able to restore page mappings without dropping PageLRU and that is unlikely to be worth maintaining.

Patch Set 2:

Should this patch be abandoned in favour of using cl_env to keep the thread state between calls, as with https://review.whamcloud.com/35700 ?

Abandoned

in favor of https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/35700/

parents.subject: LU-11617 mdc: fix possible deadlock in chlg_open()

commit.subject: LU-12578 obdecho: reduce cpu usage during obdfilter-survey
commit.message:
LU-12578 obdecho: reduce cpu usage during obdfilter-survey

Direct I/O:

Testing obdfilter-survey shows low bandwidth and an excessive
cpu utilization. perf testing indicated the page alloc / free
being the primary hot spot.

The patch alleviates the condition directly by providing a pagepool
front end to page_alloc and __page_free of the affected code paths.

With this applied cpu dropped from 98% to 30% and bandwidth
increased to estimated hardware limits.

Buffered I/O:

Using the pagepool infrastructure include page cache stealing
to avoid the page_alloc/free overhead.
Buffered I/O remains CPU bound however this provides ~20% better
CPU utilization and bandwidth.
Page cache still exhibits a couple of hot spots associated with
address space mappings and cgroup accounting.

Cray-bug-id: LUS-7552
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <stancheff@cray.com>
Change-Id: Iadcc4270b4405c1f35b11c234e38ee0a3b9cad82
